text: Code reviews are a crucial part of software development. In a code review, a developer’s code is checked by one or more teammates before it is added to the main project. The main purpose is catching bugs early, before they cause bigger problems. This saves time and money in the long run. Beyond bugs, code reviews also encourage knowledge sharing on a team. When more than one person understands the code, the team becomes stronger. If a developer leaves, others can take over their work. Overall, reviews improve both code and team quality.
